database_entries.raw_text: Remaining Note 17 items (after Mfg, Rent, Salary, Ads, Repairs, Audit allocations)
database_entries.industry_note: Others SGA is the catch-all for all Note 17 items not classified as Manufacturing, Rent, Salary, Ads, Repairs, or Audit/Dir Rem. Delivery Charges is typically the largest component in trading companies (paper distribution costs). Major components: Delivery Charges (~6.81 Cr), Travelling (~2.41 Cr), Royalty (~1.68 Cr), Professional Fees (~1.67 Cr), Processing Fee (~1.50 Cr), Commission (~0.85 Cr), Insurance (~0.49 Cr), plus ~20 smaller items

database_entries.industry_note: Negative statutory liabilities indicate excess advance payments of PF/ESI/TDS/GST. Common in companies that pre-pay statutory dues to avoid penalties.

database_entries.industry_note: Quasi Equity represents unsecured loans from promoters/directors that are treated as part of net worth in CMA analysis. This significantly improves the company apparent capital structure. Growing from 8.80 Cr to 16.44 Cr shows increasing promoter capital infusion.
database_entries.reasoning: Split from Note 4: Promoter unsecured loan portion reclassified as Quasi Equity. This is the most significant CMA reclassification, moving 8.8 Cr from Short-term Borrowings to Net Worth.

database_entries.industry_note: In this trading company, all inventory is classified as Raw Material (Indigenous) in the CMA. There is no WIP or finished goods since this is a trading (not manufacturing) operation. The inventory is paper & paper products held for resale.

database_entries.industry_note: Security Deposits (godown/warehouse rent deposits, utility deposits) shown as Short-term in statutory BS are reclassified as Non-Current in CMA. This is a standard CMA reclassification reflecting the economic reality that these deposits are typically not refundable in the short term.
database_entries.reasoning: Reclassified from Note 10 Current Assets to CMA Non-Current Assets: Security deposits (1.71 Cr). Grows significantly from Y1 to Y2 suggesting new warehouse deposits.
